content:
✨ OpenCut | TypeScript
🎯 Primary Use Case: A cross-platform, open-source video editing application.
✨ Key Features: • Editor API • Third-party plugins • Cross-platform (web, desktop, mobile) with Rust core • MCP server for AI agents • Headless mode for automation
📖 Summary: OpenCut is a free and open-source video editor being rewritten from the ground up to support web, desktop, and mobile platforms with a Rust core. It aims to offer a plugin-first architecture, an editor API, and headless capabilities for automation and batch rendering.
🔗 Links: • View Project • Homepage ================ 🔓 Open Source

🔥 CLI-Anything | Python
🎯 Primary Use Case: To enable AI agents to interact with and control any software through a unified command-line interface system.
✨ Key Features: • Agent-native software integration • CLI-Hub for browsing, installing, and managing community-built CLIs • Support for various AI agents (Pi, OpenClaw, nanobot, Cursor, Claude Code, etc.) • Extensible through community contributions
📖 Summary: CLI-Anything is a Python-based framework designed to make all software accessible to AI agents. It provides a unified hub for installing and managing command-line interfaces (CLIs), enabling AI agents to interact with and control various applications and services seamlessly.
🔗 Links: • View Project • Homepage ================ 🔓 Open Source

🔥 annyang | TypeScript
🎯 Primary Use Case: Enabling users to control websites and web applications using voice commands.
✨ Key Features: • Speech recognition for websites • Voice command control • No dependencies • Lightweight (2 KB) • MIT License
📖 Summary: annyang is a lightweight JavaScript library that enables speech recognition on websites, allowing users to control web applications and interact with content using voice commands. It requires no external dependencies and is easy to integrate.
🔗 Links: • View Project • Homepage ================ 🔓 Open Source

🚀 promises-unwrapping | JavaScript
🎯 Primary Use Case: Defining and refining the specification for JavaScript Promises to standardize asynchronous programming.
✨ Key Features: • Definition of Promise states: fulfilled, rejected, pending • Explanation of Promise resolution and settlement • Abstract operations for Promise management (PromiseCapability, PromiseReaction) • Detailed algorithm steps for Promise handling (IfAbruptRejectPromise)
📖 Summary: This repository contains the specification for ES6 Promises, defining their states (fulfilled, rejected, pending) and behavior. It serves as a staging ground for refining the promises specification and fixing related issues, aiming to provide a robust foundation for asynchronous operations in JavaScript.
🔗 Links: • View Project ================ 🔓 Open Source

✨ eidos | TypeScript
🎯 Primary Use Case: To provide a local-first, extensible, and AI-enhanced personal data management system that functions like a pocket database.
✨ Key Features: • Personal Data Management with Notion-like documents and databases • Offline Support with local data storage • AI Features for translation, summarization, and data interaction • Extensible framework with a simple extension system • Open SQLite data format
📖 Summary: Eidos is an extensible framework for personal data management, transforming SQLite into a user-friendly pocket database. It offers Notion-like document and database capabilities with strong offline support and integrated AI features for data interaction and extension generation.
🔗 Links: • View Project • Homepage ================ 🔓 Open Source
